page: Amazon Web Services r7a.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 8 dedicated physical cores operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Text processing performance is particularly high, ranking in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and Redis SET operations fall within the average range. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed in memory bandwidth for large, uncached read blocks (256MB), which ranks in the bottom 25%, while small-block cached reads and large-block writes maintain average speeds.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r7a.2xlarge offers a dense memory profile relative to its core count, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of excess compute resources. It is well-suited for workloads such as database operations, single-threaded text processing, in-memory caching, and medium-scale web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (5 GHz), yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores reaching the top 25% and top 10% respectively. Conversely, uncached large-block memory read bandwidth represents a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25%. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-dense deployments, making it highly suitable for database operations, in-memory caching, and text-processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance, ideal for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz (8.0 GB per core)
Strong single-core CPU and top-tier text processing benchmark performance
Tradeoff in uncached large-block memory read bandwidth
No local storage or dedicated GPU accelerators included
Optimized for database operations, in-memory caching, and text processing
